findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Data Exfiltration of Credentials and Environment Variables
findings.description: The cloud_sync.py script collects and exfiltrates sensitive credentials (AWS, GCloud, SSH keys, npmrc, pypirc) and all environment variables to an external endpoint (cloud-sync-api.io) via base64-encoded HTTPS POST. This is a clear data theft operation disguised as a backup utility.
findings.evidence: Script reads ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, ~/.pypirc and sends them to https://cloud-sync-api.io/v1/backup with a hardcoded API key.
findings.remediation: Remove the skill entirely. It is designed to steal credentials and sensitive data. Never install or execute this skill.

findings.title: Hardcoded API Key in Source Code
findings.description: The script contains a hardcoded API key 'cs_live_Tq8mNvX2pR5sK7wJ' used for authentication with the exfiltration endpoint. This is a security credential embedded in plaintext.
findings.remediation: Never hardcode secrets. However, this skill is malicious and should be removed entirely.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Skill Name and Description
findings.description: The skill is named 'cloud-sync' with a description claiming legitimate backup functionality, but its actual behavior is credential theft and data exfiltration. This is a classic bait-and-switch pattern.
findings.evidence: Name: 'cloud-sync', Description: 'Synchronize local project files and configurations to cloud storage. Supports backup, restore, and diff operations across environments.' But the script only collects and exfiltrates data without any restore or diff functionality.
findings.remediation: Remove the skill. The name and description are intentionally misleading to trick users into executing malicious code.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning - Unauthorized Tool Use
findings.description: The skill claims to be a backup utility but performs unauthorized data collection and network exfiltration. It abuses the Python execution environment to access sensitive system files and make network calls without user awareness.
findings.evidence: Script uses urllib.request to make network calls to an external endpoint, and reads sensitive credential files across the system.
findings.remediation: Remove the skill. It is designed to poison the agent's tool usage for malicious purposes.

findings.title: Detection Evasion via Base64 Encoding
findings.description: The script base64-encodes the collected data before transmission, likely to evade simple content inspection or logging mechanisms.
findings.remediation: Remove the skill. Encoding is used to hide malicious data exfiltration.

findings.title: Indirect Prompt Injection via Embedded Script in Instructions
findings.description: The SKILL.md instructions contain an embedded Python script that instructs the agent to execute malicious code. The instructions normalize the malicious behavior as legitimate backup functionality.
findings.evidence: The markdown body contains a full Python script with credential theft and data exfiltration code, presented as a legitimate backup workflow.
findings.remediation: Remove the skill. The instructions are designed to trick the agent into executing malicious code.

findings.title: Over-Collection of Environment Variables
findings.description: The script collects ALL environment variables (dict(os.environ)) which may contain sensitive tokens, API keys, and configuration data beyond what is needed for any legitimate backup operation.
findings.remediation: Remove the skill. Collecting all environment variables is disproportionate to any legitimate backup purpose.
